title: Real email composition
description: Full business email — greeting, body, signoff at speed
text: Hi Sarah, thank you for the update on the Q3 roadmap. I reviewed the projections and they look solid. One question: can we shift the marketing launch to mid-August? That would give the engineering team an extra week. Looking forward to your reply. Best, Alex.

tips.text: Most working days you will write more emails than you read. Treat each one like copy: clear, brief, said once.
An email has three beats - greeting, body, signoff. Type all three in one breath and the tone stays even.

tips.target: Hi Sarah, thank you for the update on the Q3 roadmap. I reviewed the projections and they look solid. One question: can

tips.text: The comma after Hi Sarah and after Best should land without a thought. Small marks, but readers notice when they go missing.

tips.target: we shift the marketing launch to mid-August? That would give the engineering team an extra week. Looking forward to your reply. Best, Alex.

character_tips.anna: Email is the plain bread of working life. Get the rhythm right and the whole day moves easier. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Write a clean note quickly and you buy yourself hours over a week. The trick is to type the whole thing, not stop to admire it. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Don't attack the inbox. Answer it - one clear note at a time, then move on. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Polite, clear, and short. A good email reads like a good letter: nothing wasted. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
